citation: Goldberg, L.R. (1999). In Mervielde et al. (Eds.), Personality psychology in Europe (Vol.7, pp.7-28). Tilburg, NL: Tilburg University Press.
also_see: Johnson, J.A. (2014). IPIP-NEO-120. J. Research in Personality, 51, 78-89.
scoring_rule: +keyed: scored_value = raw(1-5).  -keyed: scored_value = 6-raw.  Facet score = sum of 10 scored_values (range 10-50).  Domain score = sum of 6 facet scores (range 60-300).
structure: 5 domains x 6 facets x 10 items = 300 items total

Neuroticism.mbti_proxy: None — no MBTI equivalent; inverse of Emotional Stability

Extraversion.mbti_proxy: EI — high=E, low=I. Best facets: E1 Friendliness, E2 Gregariousness, E3 Assertiveness

Openness.mbti_proxy: NS — high=N, low=S. Best facets: O1 Imagination, O5 Intellect. O6 Liberalism has political content — consider omitting.

Agreeableness.mbti_proxy: TF — high Agreeableness = F, low = T (INVERTED). Best facets: A3 Altruism, A6 Sympathy

Conscientiousness.mbti_proxy: JP — high=J, low=P. Best facets: C2 Orderliness, C5 Self-Discipline

notes: MBTI proxy mappings are domain-level approximations. Individual facets vary in MBTI relevance.
For a Bayesian adaptive MBTI quiz, prioritise: E1/E2/E3 (EI), O1/O5 (NS), A3/A6 (TF), C2/C5 (JP).
The 120-item IPIP-NEO-120 selects the 4 highest-loading items per facet from this pool.
O6 Liberalism and N5 Immoderation contain sensitive items; consider omitting for general audiences.
Neuroticism has no MBTI counterpart and should be treated as a separate outcome variable.
